name: La Moleta and Ibón de Iserias Circular Trail
name_original: La Moleta – circular desde Canfranc por El Carretón y el ibón de Iserias
description: Hike the difficult La Moleta circular from Canfranc, gaining 4471 feet over 10.9 miles to panoramic views and Ibón de Iserias.
region_intro: Hiking around Canfranc offers diverse landscapes within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by majestic mountains and the historic Canfranc Valley. The region features lush forests of beech, pine, and acacia, alongside rivers and ravines like the Arones. Hikers can explore serene mountain lakes, known as "ibóns," and access several impressive peaks that provide panoramic views. This varied terrain ensures a wide array of trails for different experience levels. Best hiking trails around Canfranc The most popular hiking route is La Moleta and Ibón de Iserias Circular Trail, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 7 hours 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends through high mountain terrain to reach the picturesque Ibón de Iserias. Another top favourite among local hikers is Canfranc and Canfranc Estacion via the French Way of St. James, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail follows a segment of the historic pilgrimage route, connecting the two Canfranc settlements. Local hikers also love the Fuente de la Herradura – Bunkers of the P Line loop from Canfranc, a 6.2 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through forested areas and historical defensive structures, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Canfranc is defined by Pyrenees mountains, historic passes, and serene mountain lakes. The network offers options for both casual walkers and experienced mountaineers. The routes in Canfranc are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Canfranc's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Canfranc?
A: Canfranc offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 60 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Canfranc?
A: Hiking in Canfranc immerses you in the majestic Aragonese Pyrenees. You'll encounter diverse landscapes including lush forests of beech, pine, and acacia, deep ravines like the Arones, and serene mountain lakes known as "ibóns." The region also features impressive peaks offering panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Canfranc?
A: The hiking routes in Canfranc are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning Pyrenean mountain scenery, the variety of trails, and the opportunity to discover historic sites and beautiful natural features.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Canfranc?
A: Yes, Canfranc offers several easy and moderate trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938314676/canfranc-and-canfranc-estacion-via-the-french-way-of-st-james" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canfranc and Canfranc Estacion via the French Way of St. James&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.3-mile path that follows a historic pilgrimage route, connecting the two settlements. There are 12 easy and 22 moderate routes in total.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced mountaineers in Canfranc?
A: Absolutely. Canfranc is an excellent base for experienced mountaineers seeking challenging routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942987164/la-moleta-and-ibon-de-iserias-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moleta and Ibón de Iserias Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 10.9-mile route, ascend through high mountain terrain to reach picturesque lakes. There are 26 difficult routes available for those looking for a significant challenge.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Canfranc area?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in Canfranc. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Herradura – Bunkers of the P Line loop from Canfranc&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.2-mile circular trail that takes you through forested areas and past historical defensive structures. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942987164/la-moleta-and-ibon-de-iserias-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moleta and Ibón de Iserias Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is more challenging.

Q: Are there any mountain lakes (ibóns) I can hike to in Canfranc?
A: Yes, the region is known for its serene mountain lakes, or "ibóns." The picturesque Ibón de Iserias is a notable example, accessible via the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942987164/la-moleta-and-ibon-de-iserias-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moleta and Ibón de Iserias Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Ibón de Escalar is another beautiful lake in the area.

Q: What notable peaks can be accessed from Canfranc?
A: Canfranc provides access to several impressive peaks in the Pyrenees. The Pic des Moines, located on the border at 2,349 meters, offers breathtaking views. Other significant peaks in the vicinity include La Moleta, A Raca, Borreguil de la Cuca, and Cuyalaret, all providing panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to explore near Canfranc?
A: Yes, the area around Canfranc features several stunning waterfalls and gorges. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsaroleta Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4662298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ip Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable gorges include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culivillas Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5129372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Roya&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Canfranc?
A: Canfranc is rich in history. The monumental Canfranc International Railway Station is a significant landmark and a starting point for many hikes. You can also explore historical defensive structures along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Herradura – Bunkers of the P Line loop from Canfranc&lt;/a&gt;, which incorporates old bunkers.

Q: Is Canfranc connected to any long-distance hiking trails?
A: Yes, Canfranc serves as a gateway to major long-distance routes. It provides access to the GR11 (Senda Pirenaica), which traverses the Spanish Pyrenees, and the Camino Aragonés, a segment of the historic French Way of St. James pilgrimage route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Canfranc?
A: The best time for hiking in Canfranc generally depends on the specific trail and your experience level. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is ideal for higher altitude routes, while winter provides opportunities for snowshoeing or winter mountaineering, though some trails may be inaccessible due to snow.

Q: Are there options for parking when accessing hiking trails in Canfranc?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Canfranc, particularly near popular trailheads and the Canfranc International Railway Station, which serves as a common starting point for many routes into the surrounding Pyrenees.
tips: Steep climb on very loose ground. Definitely requires experience and should not be ventured lightly. Very rewarding view and a great feeling when you've made it up!
Nice little shelter with an oven

The Canfranc International Railway Station is a splendid building bathed in various architectural influences that was conceived as a great showcase of Spain for foreign visitors.
It is essential to book in advance at the Tourist Office, online on the website www.canfranc.es

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for hiking this circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike typically takes around 7 hours and 48 minutes to complete. However, given its difficulty and significant elevation gain, it's wise to allow extra time for breaks and enjoying the panoramic views.

faqs.question: Is the La Moleta circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners or families with young children. It requires very good fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and some alpine experience. The section to the summit of La Moleta and the Ibón de Iserias is particularly demanding.

faqs.question: Where does the La Moleta circular trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Canfranc Estación. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the Canfranc International Railway Station, which serves as a convenient starting point for many hikes in the area.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the La Moleta circular hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter a 'challenging vertical kilometer' ascent via El Carretón through dense forests. As you gain altitude, the path transitions to open, rocky sections with loose stones and narrow passages. Some parts, especially above 2,000 meters, may even feature fixed chains or 'clavijas' to aid navigation. The descent often leads through the picturesque Izas valley with green meadows.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular Pyrenean landscapes. Key highlights include the stunning, emerald-green Ibón de Iserias (also known as Ibón de Samán), a hidden glacial lake. You'll also pass by the Divina or Negras Waterfall and enjoy panoramic views from the summit of La Moleta, overlooking peaks like Midi d'Ossau and Collarada. The historical Canfranc International Railway Station is also visible from above.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike La Moleta?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the high mountain passes are clear of snow. It is not recommended in conditions of heavy snow or ice due to avalanche risk and exposure, as it requires alpine experience.

faqs.question: Is the La Moleta circular hike dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, given its 'difficult' classification, varied terrain including rocky sections and potential fixed chains, and the need for sure-footedness, it may be very challenging or unsuitable for many dogs. Always ensure your dog is fit for such demanding conditions and check local regulations regarding leashes and protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the La Moleta circular trail. However, it's always good practice to check for any updated local regulations before your trip, especially if visiting protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Canal de Izas (26%), Pista de Coll de Ladrones (14%), and Camino a Buenavista (12%). It also briefly touches upon the Aragonese Way (20%) and the Variante Tacheras – Oza – Ref. Gabardito – Ref. de Lizara – Canfranc Estación – Canal Roya (20%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes by the Refugio Huerta Seséñas, which might offer some basic facilities or shelter. While there are natural water sources like the Divina or Negras Waterfall and the Ibón de Iserias, it's crucial to carry sufficient water for a hike of this duration and difficulty, as natural sources may not always be reliable or potable.

faqs.question: What should I pack for the La Moleta circular hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and high-mountain environment, you should pack sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layers of clothing for varying weather conditions (even in summer), rain gear, plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a map and compass or GPS device, a first-aid kit, and sun protection. Trekking poles can also be very helpful on the steep and rocky sections.
